finding_results.notes: All four cited Tommy lines reworked to draw from 2005 gambling, bar, family, and neighborhood language. 'Temporary liquidity issue' became a stacked domestic pitch ending on 'Christmas is normal, done' — Tommy selling normalcy in family terms. 'Curated a strong brand' became 'coming to you with this same shit since I was twenty' — relational, age-specific, profane in Tommy's high-contraction register. 'Overall vibe' removed entirely in favor of direct concrete complaint. 'Medically compromised' became 'calling it a loss' — gambling language applied to frozen peas, a joke only Tommy would make in this book. Supporting narrator comparison at line 143 adjusted from 'wearing a headset and pitching venture capital' to 'sell the wreckage back to you as a plan that was already working' so Nate's 2021 interior comparison still tracks without claiming Tommy's actual words sound like VC language. None of the revised lines could plausibly be reassigned to Nate's interior voice. All retain Tommy's humor and desperation.
